severity: silent wrong answer — output distribution changes, no error raised
found_by: cforge-fuzz campaign via tools/fuzz_qiskit.py, N1 amplitude oracle, seed 42; 10/3000 random circuits hit it, shrinker reduced all to the same 3-gate core
expected: sxdg . sxdg = (SX-dagger)^2 = X exactly, so qubit 1 must end in |1> from |0> with probability 1
observed: CommutativeCancellation deletes both sxdg gates; qubit 1 stays |0>. transpile(qc, optimization_level=3) counts: {'00','01'} instead of {'10','11'}
standalone_reproducer_python:
from qiskit import QuantumCircuit
from qiskit.transpiler import PassManager
from qiskit.transpiler.passes import CommutativeCancellation
from qiskit.quantum_info import Operator
qc = QuantumCircuit(2)
qc.sxdg(1); qc.sx(0); qc.sxdg(1)
out = PassManager([CommutativeCancellation()]).run(qc)
print(out)                              # only sx(0) survives
print(Operator(qc).equiv(Operator(out)))  # False
notes: optimization_level 0 and 1 are correct; 2 and 3 are wrong (pass enabled at O2+)
adjacent-in-list sxdg;sxdg on the same qubit is handled CORRECTLY (merged to rx) — the bug needs an interleaved instruction on another qubit, then the pair is treated as inverse-cancelling
sx;sx is left untouched, so the self-inverse treatment is asymmetric and specific to sxdg
distinct from open issue Qiskit/qiskit#5878 (2021): that one needs a cz and commutation through it; this one has no two-qubit gate at all
n1_amplitude_distance 7.071e-01, probability-level distance 1.0 on qubit 1 — visible in counts, NOT a theorem-class bug
